qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Server-Side Request Forgery (SSRF) in Downloadable Product Sample Downloads
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The advisory describes a file upload blocklist bypass (forbidden_extensions=php,exe) in app/code/core/Mage/Catalog/Model/Product/Option/Type/File.php:230-237 leading to RCE via uploaded PHP files. The SSRF finding targets a completely different component (Mage_Downloadable_Helper_Download::_getHandle() using fsockopen()) with a different root cause (missing private IP range validation vs incomplete extension blocklist), different vulnerability class (SSRF vs unrestricted file upload CWE-434), and different impact vector (reading internal services vs executing uploaded PHP code).

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The advisory (GHSA-3j5q-7q7h-2hhv) describes an incomplete file upload extension blocklist (forbidden_extensions=php,exe) in app/code/core/Mage/Catalog/Model/Product/Option/Type/File.php:230-237 that allows uploading PHP-executable files (.phtml, .phar, .php3, etc.) to the publicly accessible media/custom_options/quote/ directory, enabling Remote Code Execution (CWE-434). The only qualifying finding (ssrf-downloadable-product) describes an SSRF vulnerability in Mage_Downloadable_Helper_Download::_getHandle() using fsockopen() without private IP range validation — a different component, different root cause, and different vulnerability class. No qualifying finding identifies the advisory-described vulnerable code or root cause.
evidence: Advisory vulnerable code: app/code/core/Mage/Catalog/Model/Product/Option/Type/File.php:230-237 (_validateUploadedFile() with incomplete ExcludeExtension validator)
Advisory root cause: forbidden_extensions=php,exe blocklist misses .phtml, .phar, .php3, .php4, .php5, .php7, .pht
Advisory CWE: CWE-434 (Unrestricted Upload of File with Dangerous Type)
Qualifying finding root cause: fsockopen() without private IP range validation
Qualifying finding vulnerability class: SSRF, not file upload bypass
